methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $5.3B.

data.narrative:
Freenome, a public-stage biotech and health company founded by Gabriel Otte, is evaluated against a peer universe of 11 comparable firms (3 public, 8 private) in the same sector and valuation tier. The company has raised $1.3B+ and currently trades at $1.1B.

Peer-median valuation represents the applicable triangulation method. Across six same-sector comparables—including Abridge ($5.3B, Series E), Chai Discovery ($3.8B, Series B), New Limit ($3.1B, Series C), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.6B, public), and Alto Neuroscience ($1.0B, public)—the median valuation is $2.4B, with a range spanning $539M to $5.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied; while the Biotech & Health sector median P/S across three public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) is 6.9x, Freenome's revenue is not disclosed and thus multiples cannot be computed.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.7B (median $2.4B), implying the current $1.1B valuation sits materially below peer median. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and clinical-stage progress, both unavailable in the dataset. The single-method triangulation limits confidence; additional revenue or comparable transaction data would strengthen the analysis.
